data.executiveSummary: The qa-login service experienced a high error rate of 31.66% due to Redis connection timeouts impacting user sessions.

rootCause.title: Redis Connection Timeout
rootCause.description: The incident was caused by persistent connection timeouts with the Redis service, leading to session store retrieval failures in the qa-login service. This issue resulted in a high rate of login exceptions, as observed in the logs.

evidenceChain: High error rate of 31.66% detected for the qa-login service.
Loki logs show repeated 'LoginException: Session store retrieval failure. Redis connection timeout after 5000ms.' errors.
CPU usage was at 29.20%, and memory usage was high at 91.43%, which may have contributed to resource exhaustion and connection timeouts.

timeline.event: First appearance of 'LoginException: Session store retrieval failure' errors in logs.
Continued occurrence of the same error indicating ongoing connection issues with Redis.

data.impact: Users experienced delays and failures when attempting to log in, as the service was unable to retrieve session data due to Redis connection timeouts.

similarIncident.title: Redis Connection Timeout Spikes
similarIncident.resolution: Resolution: Restarted redis-primary-3 instance due to orphaned connections.

recommendedActions: Restart the affected Redis instance to clear potential orphaned connections causing the timeout.
Investigate and increase the connection pool size or timeout threshold for Redis in qa-login configuration.
Implement comprehensive monitoring and alerting for unusual resource usage similar to historical incident KAN-6, to prevent future occurrences.
